_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: LOCAL batch — San Antonio TX City Council (D1 Kaur, D5 Castillo) + San Marcos TX City Council/Mayor (Garza P3, Rodriguez P6, Hughson Mayor). Evidence-based finalization, LOCAL rubric. Roster/candidacy web-verified June 2026. reset_unspecified: scores reset to null where no documented evidence; FALSE only on a cited vote/proclamation/statement; TRUE held to the high bar (affirmative advocacy, not routine governance). Left-coded local-control invocations (anti-state-preemption for progressive ends) left null on refuse_state_overreach rather than mis-credited. Adds v5.0 local categories (public_justice, refuse_state_overreach) where records predate them.

profile.religion: Sikh (first Sikh American on San Antonio City Council; not Christian)
profile.education: EdD K-12 Educational Leadership (Vanderbilt); MBA (Rice); BA Policy Studies (Rice)
profile.background: Education consultant / 16-yr public-school educator; CEO-founder EDreimagined. D1 council member; led May 3 2025 field, won June 7 2025 runoff 64.99%, now a 4-yr term.

sukh-kaur.notes_set: [2026-06-17 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Candidacy web-verified: sitting D1 council member, won June 7 2025 runoff (64.99%), 4-yr term. HARD anti-rubric findings: voted YES (council 6-5, Apr 3 2025) to fast-track $100k to the Reproductive Justice Fund including out-of-state abortion travel, and stated 'access to comprehensive healthcare, including reproductive care, is fundamental' (sanctity_of_life FALSE). Co-supported San Antonio's first Trans History Week proclamation and pushed to add the transgender flag to replacement Pride sidewalks after the state forced removal of the rainbow crosswalk (biblical_marriage q2,q4 FALSE). Signaled openness to a property-tax hike amid the FY2027 deficit, preferring the higher-spending scenario (economic_stewardship FALSE). Organized the Jan 22 2026 council session on ICE, worked to set up an immigrant legal-defense fund, observed immigration-court hearings, opposed a proposed ICE detention facility (border_immigration q1,q2 FALSE). Sikh, not Christian — no Christian-liberty record. Police record is reform-leaning/mixed ('supports police but holds accountable', retired officers for low-priority calls) — does not meet the affirmative pro-police high bar, left null. NO EVIDENCE FOUND on schools curriculum/parental rights, election integrity, or guns -> null.

0.note: Voted to fund out-of-state abortion travel via the Reproductive Justice Fund; called reproductive care 'fundamental' — affirmatively pro-abortion-access, contrary to affirming personhood at conception.

1.note: Voted to FUND abortion access (travel) — the opposite of advocating abortion abolition.

2.note: Co-supported Trans History Week and pushed to incorporate the transgender flag into city Pride sidewalks — affirms, not rejects, transgender ideology.

4.note: Championed Pride/transgender-flag sidewalks as city policy — promotes LGBTQ identity in public policy.

2.note: During FY2027 deficit talks said she 'wouldn't be opposed to a tax hike' and preferred the higher-spending budget scenario.

1.note: Organized council action against ICE coordination and an immigrant legal-defense fund — opposes deportation enforcement.

2.note: Opposed an ICE detention facility and SAPD-ICE coordination — pro-sanctuary, against federal immigration cooperation.

profile.background: Self-identified DSA member; former housing organizer (Texas Organizing Project) and SAISD educator. D5 council member since June 2021; re-elected May 2025 (~77%), term to 2029.

teri-castillo.notes_set: [2026-06-17 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Candidacy web-verified: sitting D5 council member since June 2021, re-elected May 3 2025 (~77%), term to 2029; self-identified DSA. HARD anti-rubric findings: sponsored the non-binding abortion resolution (council 9-2, Aug 2022) and backed the 2023 Prop A 'Justice Charter' decriminalizing abortion + marijuana and expanding cite-and-release (sanctity_of_life FALSE; public_justice q1 FALSE — soft-on-crime). Supported San Antonio's first Trans History Week ('trans history is civil rights history') (biblical_marriage q2,q4 FALSE). Consistently on the police-reallocation side: opposed adding up to 50 SAPD officers, voted to cut a police-budget increase, signed a 2026 memo against expanded police staffing (public_justice q0 FALSE). At the Jan 22 2026 ICE session said 'ICE has no place in San Antonio' and called it 'textbook fascism' (border_immigration q1,q2 FALSE). No clean budget/tax-rate vote isolatable -> economic left null (housing-spending advocacy not scored as a balanced-budget position). Anti-state-preemption (HB2127 'Death Star' lawsuit) is left-coded local control -> refuse_state_overreach null, not credited. NO EVIDENCE on schools, Christian liberty, elections, guns -> null.

0.note: Sponsored the council abortion resolution (fund usable for out-of-state abortion travel) and backed Prop A abortion decriminalization — affirmatively pro-abortion.

1.note: Sponsored funding for abortion access — opposite of advocating abortion abolition.

2.note: Supported Trans History Week, stating 'trans history is civil rights history' — affirms transgender ideology.

4.note: Backed the first Trans History Week proclamation as city recognition — promotes LGBTQ identity in public policy.

0.note: Opposed adding up to 50 SAPD officers, voted to cut a police-budget increase, and signed a 2026 memo against expanded police staffing — police-reallocation side, not backing law enforcement.

1.note: Backed the Prop A 'Justice Charter' expanding cite-and-release / decriminalization — soft-on-crime posture.
Said 'ICE has no place in San Antonio'; opposes immigration enforcement/deportation.

2.note: Opposes SAPD-ICE cooperation — pro-sanctuary, against federal immigration cooperation.

profile.background: Professional organizer at Mano Amiga (criminal-justice/police-accountability group): Community Outreach Director then Public Health & Safety Director. Council Place 3 since Nov 2020, re-elected 2022, now Mayor Pro Tem; term 2023-2026 (up Nov 2026).

alyssa-garza.notes_set: [2026-06-17 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Candidacy/title web-verified: sitting Place 3 council member, now Mayor Pro Tem (office corrected from 'Deputy Mayor Pro Tem'); term 2023-2026, up Nov 2026. San Marcos council is officially nonpartisan; documented record is progressive (party left Unknown — not inferred). HARD anti-rubric findings: as a Mano Amiga organizer she championed the 2022 marijuana-decriminalization Prop A and pressed police-accountability changes that forced repeal of the police-union contract (public_justice q0,q1 FALSE). Backed the 'ICE Out of Hays County' effort demanding the sheriff refuse a 287(g) agreement (border_immigration q1,q2 FALSE). Aligned with the consensus to keep abortion enforcement de-prioritized but no clear personal life-at-conception statement -> sanctity_of_life null. Her NO votes on budgets/tax-rate are a documented LEFT critique (spending priorities), not balanced-budget opposition -> economic_stewardship null, not credited. Local-control marijuana stance vs state preemption is left-coded -> refuse_state_overreach null. NO EVIDENCE on marriage/LGBT, schools, Christian liberty, elections, guns -> null.

0.note: Mano Amiga police-accountability organizer who pressed changes that forced repeal of the police-union contract — does not back law enforcement; opposes the policing status quo.

1.note: Championed the 2022 marijuana-decriminalization Prop A — soft-on-crime/decriminalization posture.
Opposed ICE operations (as Mano Amiga Public Health & Safety Director) — against deportation enforcement.

2.note: Backed 'ICE Out of Hays County' demanding the sheriff refuse a 287(g) agreement — pro-sanctuary/non-cooperation.

profile.background: Lifelong San Marcos resident; former Texas Capitol legislative/policy staffer; ex-board president Centro Cultural Hispano de San Marcos; community organizer. Council Place 6, elected Nov 5 2024 (64.16%), first term 2024-2027.

amanda-rodriguez.notes_set: [2026-06-17 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Candidacy web-verified: sitting Place 6 council member, elected Nov 5 2024 (64.16%), first term 2024-2027 (disambiguated as the San Marcos TX councilmember). Nonpartisan office; documented record is progressive (party left Unknown — not inferred). Thin rubric-mappable record. HARD finding: voted FOR raising the property-tax rate from 60.30c to 65.15c per $100 for FY2026 (economic_stewardship FALSE). GRACE Act predates her tenure (no vote) -> sanctity_of_life null, not inferred. Police-reform/Mano Amiga-aligned and spoke against police conduct re the Malachi Williams killing, but explicitly rejected 'defund' framing and made no affirmative pro-police statement -> public_justice null (neither clean TRUE nor FALSE). Pro-immigrant lean is SOFT with no HARD city action -> border null. Gaza-ceasefire co-sponsorship vs Abbott funding threat is foreign-policy/left local-control -> refuse_state_overreach null. NO EVIDENCE on marriage/LGBT, schools, Christian liberty, elections, guns -> null.

2.note: Voted FOR raising the San Marcos property-tax rate from 60.30c to 65.15c per $100 for FY2026.

profile.education: Southwest Texas State (now Texas State University)
profile.background: Retired from Texas State University after 33 years. Mayor of San Marcos since 2018 (re-elected 2020, 2022, 2024; 2-yr terms, current term to 2026); prior council service 1996-2002 and 2014-2017. CAPCOG Executive Committee; Hays Central Appraisal District.

jane-hughson.notes_set: [2026-06-17 RESOLUTE LOCAL refinement] Candidacy web-verified: sitting Mayor since 2018, re-elected through Nov 2024, current term to 2026 (up Nov 2026). HARD anti-rubric findings: as mayor she has issued/read recurring Pride proclamations — 'San Marcos Pride Day' (Sept 2019), SMTX Pride 2024, and June 2025 LGBTQ Pride Month 'urging citizens to promote tolerance, justice, and appreciation for diversity' (biblical_marriage q4 FALSE). Presided over and defended budgets carrying effective tax increases (city has not set a rate at/below no-new-revenue since 2013; FY2025 was the highest budget in a decade) (economic_stewardship FALSE). Presided over an Aug 2019 resolution forbidding concealed handguns in government-meeting rooms — a local carry restriction (self_defense q0 FALSE). Police record is routine budget staffing (incl. mental-health/homeless-outreach officers) — does not meet the affirmative pro-police high bar -> public_justice null. GRACE Act was an SMPD directive, and marijuana Prop A was a voter initiative — neither a Hughson council vote -> sanctity_of_life / public_justice on those left null. San Marcos did NOT join the HB2127 'Death Star' lawsuit -> refuse_state_overreach NO EVIDENCE -> null. NO EVIDENCE on schools, Christian liberty, elections, immigration -> null.

4.note: As mayor issued/read recurring Pride proclamations (2019 Pride Day; June 2025 LGBTQ Pride Month urging citizens to promote diversity) — promotes LGBTQ identity in public policy.

2.note: Presided over and defended budgets with effective tax increases (rate not at/below no-new-revenue since 2013; FY2025 highest budget in a decade).

0.note: Presided over an Aug 2019 resolution forbidding concealed handguns in rooms hosting government meetings — a local carry restriction, not constitutional-carry support.
